name: Apple-Filled Chocolate Coffee Cake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsweetened Cocoa Powder, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle yeast and 1/2 tsp sugar over warm water in a glass measuring cup; stir and set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine butter, milk, remaining 1/2 cup sugar, cocoa, salt, and eggs. Beat with electric mixer on low speed. Add 3 cups flour and beat well. Beat in yeast mixture until well mixed. If your mixer has a dough hook, remove beater and attach dough hook; add remaining 2 to 2 1/2 cups flour to make a soft dough. Knead on medium-high speed 5 to 7 minutes. If you do not have a dough hook, remove dough to a lightly floured surface and knead by hand with remaining flour. Continue kneading about 10 minutes until dough is smooth and elastic. Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
3. Punch down dough. On a lightly floured pastry cloth or board, roll out dough to a 14 x 20-inch rectangle. Spread with Apple Filling to within 1 inch of edges. Starting from one long side, roll up tightly jelly-roll fashion, pinching seam and ends to seal. Place in a lightly greased 10-inch tube pan and pinch ends together to seal. Let rise until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
4. About 15 minutes before rising has been completed, preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake for 1 hour, or until golden brown. Let cool in pan for 10 minutes, then invert onto a wire rack. Serve warm or at room temperature.
APPLE FILLING
3 Tbsp Butter
2 Green Apples, peeled, cored, and cut into 1/2-inch pieces.
1/2 cup Pecans, chopped
1/2 cup Packed Brown Sugar
1/4 cup Packed Unsweetened Cocoa Powder
1 Tsp Ground Cinnamon
6 oz Semisweet Chocolate Chips
1. In a medium skillet, melt butter over low heat. Add apples and pecans and cook, stirring constantly, over high heat for 2 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. When cool, mix in brown sugar, cocoa, cinnamon, raisins, and chocolate chips.
